description: Fix documentation code examples that fail to compile when copied into a real plugin project. Discovered through execution-based testing in a clean Docker environment: scaffolding a plugin, copying doc code verbatim, and running build/type-check/lint.
qualityChecks: cd docs && npm run build && npm run type-check && npm run lint && npm run knip && npm run format:check

userStories.title: Fix resources-prompts.mdx Full Plugin Example: export class vs instance
userStories.description: As a plugin developer following the Resources & Prompts guide, I want the Full Plugin Example to export a plugin instance (not a class) so that I can copy the code and have it build successfully.
acceptanceCriteria: The Full Plugin Example code block in resources-prompts.mdx exports an instance: the class declaration does NOT use `export default class`, and a `export default new TrackerPlugin()` line follows the class
The pattern matches the first-plugin.mdx example which correctly exports `export default new MyAppPlugin()`
cd docs && npm run build passes
cd docs && npm run type-check passes
cd docs && npm run lint passes
cd docs && npm run knip passes
cd docs && npm run format:check passes
userStories.notes:
File: docs/content/docs/guides/resources-prompts.mdx, line ~511. The code block currently reads `export default class TrackerPlugin extends OpenTabsPlugin {`. This exports the CLASS, not an INSTANCE. When a developer copies this into a plugin project and runs `npm run build`, the build tool loads the module and calls `.tools.length` on what it expects to be an instance. Since it gets a class constructor instead, `tools` is `undefined` and the build crashes with 'Cannot read properties of undefined (reading "length")'.

Fix: Change `export default class TrackerPlugin` to `class TrackerPlugin` and add `export default new TrackerPlugin();` after the closing brace. This matches the pattern used in first-plugin.mdx (line ~60) and plugin-development.mdx which both correctly export instances.

For reference, the scaffolded plugin also exports an instance: `export default new MyPluginPlugin();`

userStories.title: Fix plugin-development.mdx fetchJSON examples to handle undefined return
userStories.description: As a plugin developer following the Plugin Development guide, I want the fetchJSON code examples to handle the `undefined` return case so that I can copy the code and have it pass type-check with strict mode.
acceptanceCriteria: The 'Fetch with session cookies' code block (fetchJSON without schema) includes a null check on the return value before accessing properties (e.g., `if (!data) throw ToolError.internal('...');`)
The postJSON example similarly handles the undefined case
The pattern matches the 'Putting it together' section later in the same file (line ~431) which already correctly checks `if (!data)`
cd docs && npm run build passes
cd docs && npm run type-check passes
cd docs && npm run lint passes
cd docs && npm run knip passes
cd docs && npm run format:check passes
userStories.notes:
File: docs/content/docs/guides/plugin-development.mdx, lines ~265-275. The 'Fetch with session cookies' section shows:

```
const data = await fetchJSON<{ items: Item[] }>("/api/items?limit=20");
```

and

```
const result = await postJSON<{ id: string }>("/api/items", { title: "New item", status: "active" });
```

The SDK's `fetchJSON<T>` (without a Zod schema as 3rd arg) returns `Promise<T | undefined>` — it returns `undefined` for 204 No Content responses. The scaffolded plugin's tsconfig includes `strict: true`, so accessing `data.items` or `result.id` without checking for `undefined` fails type-check with TS18048: 'data' is possibly 'undefined'.

Fix: Add `if (!data) throw ToolError.internal('...');` after each fetchJSON/postJSON call (before accessing properties). Import ToolError if not already imported. The 'Putting it together' section at line ~431 in the same file already demonstrates the correct pattern: `if (!data) throw ToolError.internal('No data returned');`

IMPORTANT: The fetchJSON WITH a Zod schema (3rd argument, line ~329) returns `Promise<z.infer<T>>` (NOT `T | undefined`) — do NOT add a null check to the schema-validated examples. Only the non-schema overload returns `T | undefined`.
